title: Kaffee vs. Coffee - Quick Summary
items: **Kaffee means the drink:** in German, Kaffee is coffee, while Café usually means the place where you drink it.
**Germany is serious about coffee:** the German Coffee Association says Germans consume around **161 litres per person per year**.
**Eiskaffee is not iced coffee:** in a traditional German café, it usually means cold coffee with vanilla ice cream and whipped cream.
**Kaffee und Kuchen is a ritual:** afternoon coffee and cake is about slowing down, sitting together and taking the pause seriously.
**Berlin has several café worlds:** old-school Konditoreien, bakeries, brunch cafés and specialty roasters all belong to the city.
